title: Pawn Wedge Mate
setup: Your pawn on **b6** plugs the {{c7}} escape, while Black's own pawns lock the **a-** and **b-files**.
hint: Storm the rook to the 8th rank — the pawn on **b6** seals {{c7}} and Black's pawns hold {{a7}} and {{b7}}.
explanation:
## The Idea

A **back-rank mate with a quiet pawn assist**. The pawn on **b6** isn't checking — but it controls c7, the only flight square the rook doesn't reach.

Play {{Rg8#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{a8}}, {{c8}} and the rest of the 8th rank — all swept by the rook on g8
- {{a7}} — blocked by Black's own a-pawn (and also attacked by the white b6 pawn)
- {{b7}} — blocked by Black's own b-pawn
- {{c7}} — controlled by the white pawn on **b6**
- The rook is too far for {{Kxg8}}

> **Takeaway:** A pawn one square in front of the enemy king's *escape file* is a hidden mate-helper. When you spot a king stuck behind two pawns, scan for tiny pawn-supports that close the third escape square.
